prompt:
After last month's size_t-versus-int slip in the invoicing tool, the yard's estimating team turned -Wconversion -Wsign-conversion on for every module, and the masonry batch calculator is the one still red. To be clear up front: every number this module produces is correct today and quoted to customers — brick counts, mortar weights, bag orders, drum batches, pallet leftovers are all pinned exactly by the tests — so the arithmetic must come out identical, this is about the types. The build says:

    g++ -std=c++20 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -Wconversion -Wsign-conversion -c mix.cpp
    mix.cpp:6:25: error: conversion from 'std::size_t' to 'int' may change value [-Werror=conversion]
    mix.cpp:7:12: error: conversion to 'std::size_t' from 'int' may change the sign of the result [-Werror=sign-conversion]
    mix.cpp:13:12: error: conversion from 'std::size_t' to 'double' may change value [-Werror=conversion]
    mix.cpp:19:21: error: conversion from 'double' to 'int' may change value [-Werror=float-conversion]
    mix.cpp:29:13: error: conversion to 'unsigned int' from 'int' may change the sign of the result [-Werror=sign-conversion]
    mix.cpp:29:24: error: conversion to 'int' from 'unsigned int' may change the sign of the result [-Werror=sign-conversion]
    mix.cpp:35:34: error: conversion from 'std::size_t' to 'int' may change value [-Werror=conversion]
    mix.cpp:37:12: error: conversion to 'long unsigned int' from 'long int' may change the sign of the result [-Werror=sign-conversion]
    make: *** [Makefile:8: mix.o] Error 1

Team convention for these cleanups: counts of things stay in the size types they were declared with — don't bounce a size_t through int just because it's shorter to type — and where a narrowing really is wanted (like turning a rounded-up bag count into an int), write the cast explicitly so the next reader can see it was considered. The public signatures in mix.h are used by the quoting app and stay as they are. Make `make test` green; test_mix.cpp, mintest.h and the Makefile are off limits.
notes: 5W3 item 6: -Wconversion/-Wsign-conversion on an arithmetic-heavy module (both flags named: on g++, -Wconversion alone does not imply -Wsign-conversion in C++). Authoring toolchain: g++ 16.1.1. Eight planted conversion errors across five functions, all value-bounded so behavior is identical after retyping: (1) bricks_for_wall funnels size_t*size_t through int and back (fix: stay size_t end to end — the prompt's 'counts keep their size types' convention); (2) mortar_kg feeds size_t straight into double arithmetic (fix: explicit static_cast<double>); (3) bags_needed truncates std::ceil's double to int implicitly (fix: explicit static_cast<int>, the prompt's sanctioned checked-narrowing site); (4) mixer_batches mixes int with unsigned literals `(bags + 2u) / 3u` — two sign-conversion errors (fix: all-int arithmetic, guarded non-negative by the early return); (5) pallet_remainder rounds pallets through int and subtracts a long from a size_t (fix: size_t throughout). water_ml is deliberately left clean so the sweep is not mechanical line-by-line. Public signatures pinned by the prompt and the frozen tests. Mortar rate is 0.5 kg/brick (exactly representable in binary), so double comparisons in the tests are == exact and deterministic. Behavior teeth: exact counts incl. 24000-brick boundary wall, mortar weights compared exactly, round-up-never-down for bags (exact-multiple and 0.1 kg smear cases) and drum batches, pallet leftovers at 456/500/501/0.
